# m/x's test imports m/y and m/y's test imports m/x.
# When rf loads tests, it creates graphs for each
# test when there are test variants of the test's dependencies.
# rf tries to merge the graphs but it can't in this case
# because there would be an import cycle, which means
# there are multiple snapshots.
# Test the case of moving a function to another package
# when there are multiple snapshots.
mv F m/q
-- m.go --
package m

import "m/q"

func F() int {
	return q.Q
}
-- q/q.go --
package q

var Q int
-- x/x.go --
package x

import "m/q"

var X = q.Q
-- x/x_test.go --
package x

import _ "m/y"
-- y/y.go --
package y

import "m/q"

var Y = q.Q
-- y/y_test.go --
package y

import _ "m/x"
-- stdout --
diff old/m.go new/m.go
--- old/m.go
+++ new/m.go
@@ -1,7 +1 @@
 package m
-
-import "m/q"
-
-func F() int {
-	return q.Q
-}
diff old/q/m.go new/q/m.go
--- old/q/m.go
+++ new/q/m.go
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+package q
+
+func F() int {
+	return Q
+}
